data.narrative:
OroraTech is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised €38M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of four private peers at identical stage and sector (Aalyria, Stoke Space, Hubble Network, and Sift), with valuations ranging from $274M to $1.3B. Five public sector comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) were identified but remain inapplicable pending revenue disclosure.

The peer-median method anchors the primary valuation. The four private comparables yield a median valuation of $700M, with a range floor of $274M (Sift) and ceiling of $1.3B (Aalyria). This method directly triangulates valuation across same-stage, same-sector peers and represents the only applicable quantitative approach given OroraTech's status as a private company without disclosed revenue. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 45.3x across six public tickers) cannot be applied without the target's revenue figure, a standard constraint in private company analysis.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $595M to $805M, centered on $700M. This range reflects peer-median positioning with a modest band around the central estimate. Key uncertainties include OroraTech's revenue scale, customer concentration, and technological differentiation relative to the peer set. The relatively wide peer range ($274M–$1.3B) signals material dispersion in Space & Aerospace Series B valuations, suggesting stage-specific risk and market segment variation warrant further due diligence on comparable business model alignment.
